item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, punchy, and features a rare good use of the 'file not found' trick. We really don't need to know what the arrangement is, that would bog down the article and kill the mystery. Is the Foundation behind the Battalion? Has it just made a devil's deal with an anomaly? Is it unable to stop the anomaly and is agreeing to hush it up for fear of retaliation? What's are the children fighting? It's better, in my opinion, that all this is left unclear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me, there's not enough - I can appreciate the twist of the SCP Foundation somehow using the anomaly to use kids, or finding the truth too horrific to be released to general Foundation knowledge - but with a dearth of crumbs, it feels - and I apologize for not being able to find a better word - lazy. As said by Baubius, there's not enough to really warrant a sense of dread or anger - too much is left in the air. I know the best ingredient to horror is a reader's imagination, but it must take some prodding.

-1, from me.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
This is a short, sweet, and I love the mystery with the object class and Attendant NR.

https://youtu.be/m-bGa1QOqz4

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Were you hoping for Ilvermorny or Hogwarts?

item.title: Re: Hmm
item.content:encoded: 0-point font, I'm guessing. There appears to be a space after "Keter": when I highlight and right-click, "Look up Thaumiel" pops up in the menu.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is NOT the letter I was expecting on my 11th birthday.

item.title: Hmm
item.content:encoded: I don’t know if anyone else has found this, but if you copy the article and paste it somewhere, the object class changes to Thaumiel. Innnnteresting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with you, I clicked on the expandable without really reading it and was briefly confused, but when I closed it and read the filename the penny dropped. Good stuff.

edit: I thought it didn't post the first time. Sorry >//<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with you, I clicked the expandable without really reading it and was briefly confused, but when I closed it and read the filename the penny dropped. Good stuff.

item.title: Some Theorizing
item.content:encoded:
Hmm… SCP-2212 is the radioactive dude who corrupts stuff (if I understood it correctly). The use of "EoI-2212" implies that it's not SCP-2212, but an entity vaguely related to it. Now, this is a shot in the dark, but maybe it's either SCP-2212's creator, or something indirectly responsible for data corruption? In both cases, these can be traced back to the author of the SCP itself.

This is a brilliant meta SCP about how many people are killed by authors in the SCPs they write. Or I'm just bad at drawing conclusions.

Either way, I enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I was enjoying this until the ending, which seems like another example of the Foundation doing bad stuff for shock value trope. I don't mind articles about how the Foundation are immoral, but the simplicity and length of this don't explore the background enough to stop it coming across as Saturday morning cartoon villainy. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite like this, even if there isn't a definite resolution as yet. Although the filename for the redacted transcript suggests a link to SCP 2212.

Has a vague "Torchwood: Children of Earth" feel to it, and harks back to the (apparently largely mythical) Childrens Crusade of the Middle Ages.

Suggests the O5's have done a Deal With The Devil to supply child soldiers to an extra-dimensional power. Whether this is to prevent the war they're fighting in coming to our Earth, or because they're benefiting from the trade in some way is uncertain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Attendant NR has been formally reprimanded… AND INFORMALLY PAID OFF! Oh oh. I said the quiet part loud, and the loud part quiet.

Seriously, why the heck are the O5s recruiting child soldiers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something weird and devious going on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough! I felt like the actual content of the file wouldn't be as important as the name, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first it had a bit of a thing going, but the ending ruined everything for me. The fact that there is no way to access this transcript is disappointing, and nothing had particularly grabbed me so far. -1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Westrin, CadaverCommander, Varaxous, MaliceAF and Modern_Erasmus for their feedback in chat!
